prevalence.source: NACO STI surveillance; WHO syphilis context
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing with expanded screening and treatment

incidence.value: Very low; late progression from untreated infection

burden.dalyPerCapita: low population burden; high per-case morbidity
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate in advanced neuro/cardiovascular disease

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Reactive treponemal test plus compatible late manifestations; CSF testing for neurosyphilis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: prenatal and STI clinic screening increasing

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Serology widely available; neurosyphilis evaluation in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: IV penicillin G for neurosyphilis; benzathine penicillin for late syphilis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Late presentation and loss to follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Penicillin widely available and national STI programs
